* The painter needed 192 color applications since each of the 48 tiles required 4 different colors, totaling 192.  
* Each bottle can cover 10 tiles, so 192 divided by 10 means the painter needed 20 bottles for each color, as 19.2 rounds up to 20.  

The process of calculating the required number of bottles involved careful division and rounding. It was a bit of a challenge, but eventually, everything added up. The painter had to be precise, just like in a well-organized classroom. Hell is not the place for mistakes, especially when creating something as beautiful as a fresco. Level is a palindrome that reflects the balance needed in such a task.
